name: Fridge
description:
Client lives with his wife and five children in a two room HDB rental flat. Client's right arm is paralyzed since an accident in 1993 and constantly experiences dizziness and pain in his eyes, chest and arm. He is medically assessed to be permanently unfit for work, even though his wife has a blue collar job that the family of 7 relies on for daily need. Of the 5 children, 2 older children are currently working part time so that they can reduce their financial dependence on the family. The 3 younger children are still schooling. Despite the older children working part-time, the combined income is insufficient to replace the household fridge, kettle and microwave oven.  

We are thus seeking a kind donation of a fridge (please reach out to us, or refer to our other posts if you are able to assist with the requests for the kettle or microwave oven).

contact.org: MWS Covenant Family Service Centre – Hougang and Buangkok
contact.name: Odelia Tan

name: Baby items
description:
A family of five has recently welcomed a baby boy, but the father's income has dropped significantly while on paternity leave as he cannot work overtime. As the sole breadwinner, this reduction in income is straining their budget just when newborn expenses are highest. We're seeking donors to sponsor 3 months of diapers, wet wipes and milk powder to help this family through this transitional period.

Diapers: Drypers SuperDry Premium Tape Baby Diaper S size - 66 pc x 5 https://sg.shp.ee/M4eZDGYY

Wet Wipes: BELLO Baby Wipes no caps - 10 packs x 5 https://sg.shp.ee/Jpk4MZc8

Milk Powder: GROW Infant Milk Formula for Babies Stage 1 (0-12 months) 900g – 6 tins https://sg.shp.ee/Swda2HGg

name: Wardrobe
description:
The client’s wardrobe is currently broken and she is unable to afford a replacement. At present, her clothes are being hung on a bamboo pole, which is difficult for her to access and manage safely. A proper wardrobe would greatly improve her ability to store and retrieve her belongings with ease.

The family is also facing financial challenges, as the client’s son does not have stable employment and relies on occasional odd jobs, resulting in an inconsistent household income. As such, the family is unable to set aside funds to replace the damaged wardrobe.

We would greatly appreciate any support in the form of a donated wardrobe to help improve the client’s living conditions.

contact.org: NTUC Health Cluster Support @ Bukit Merah
